fragility_points: BTC can move several percent in a short period, compressing the current cushion.
Small residual source-of-truth ambiguity remains because the rules name the Binance UI candle.
key_assumptions: BTC remains broadly above 72k into April 20 rather than only temporarily above it today.
No sharp selloff hits the exact relevant noon ET settlement minute.
Binance UI minute-candle behavior aligns with the documented kline interpretation.
main_logical_chain: The market resolves on the Binance BTCUSDT 12:00 ET one-minute close on April 20, so contract interpretation and timing are central.
BTC is currently trading materially above 72,000 on the designated exchange, which makes Yes the base case.
But the contract is narrow and path-dependent, so a confidence discount is warranted versus a broad directional BTC view.
That leads to a Yes estimate below the market's 84.5% implied probability.
main_thesis: BTC is more likely than not to finish above 72,000 on the relevant April 20 Binance noon ET minute close, but the market is somewhat overconfident because exact-minute path risk is underpriced.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to Yes if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone has a final Close price higher than the price specified.
Klines are uniquely identified by their open time.
source_quality_view: Source quality is medium-high overall: Binance is strong primary context for minute-candle mechanics and live price, while Polymarket is necessary for contract wording; independence is medium and source-of-truth ambiguity is low-medium.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract can fail on one bad minute because only the exact 12:00 ET Binance 1-minute close matters.
Binance's 24-hour low around 73.5k shows the current cushion is meaningful but not huge relative to normal BTC volatility.
Rules cite the Binance UI candle display rather than the API directly, leaving minor operational ambiguity.
strongest_supports: Live Binance BTCUSDT was around 75.1k at capture time, roughly 3k above the threshold.
Only several days remain to resolution, which favors the side already above strike absent a major shock.
Nearby Polymarket strike prices form a coherent probability ladder around the threshold.
timing_relevance: Very high: the contract is decided by the single Binance BTCUSDT 12:00 ET one-minute candle close on 2026-04-20, which maps to 16:00 UTC.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether realized volatility stays low enough that the noon-minute close remains comfortably above the threshold.
Whether any Binance UI-versus-API minute-candle edge case appears near settlement.
what_would_change_view: A move back toward or below 72k before April 20, higher realized volatility around the threshold, or evidence that Binance minute-candle display behavior differs from the assumed interpretation would push me away from Yes; sustained trading above roughly 74.5k-75k into April 19-20 would move me closer to the market.
